statements.status_notes: The in-process protected fetch client validates every redirect and every direct connection-time resolved IP. TestProtectedFetchClientRejectsRedirectToPrivateAddress and the protected dialer rebinding tests lock this backport; configured HTTP proxies and external Workers remain explicit operator trust boundaries.

Token search escapes literal underscores, rejects abusive percent patterns, rate-limits authenticated callers, caps pages at 100 records, caps offsets, and counts through a bounded derived query. TestSearchUserTokensEscapesWildcardsAndCapsResultPages locks the backport.

The Default renderer passes generated HTML through DOMPurify before its HTML sink. The Classic preview uses a sandbox containing only allow-same-origin; MarkdownRenderer.test.jsx locks out script, form, popup, and top-navigation permissions.

Resolved-domain IP filtering is enabled by default and the in-process webhook, Bark, Gotify, and download paths use the protected direct client for connection-time validation. The default-setting and private-rebinding tests lock this backport; configured proxies and Workers are operator trust boundaries.

Stripe callbacks fail closed without an active signing secret, require a retrieved paid Checkout Session with exact account, mode, trade, amount, currency, PaymentIntent, and customer binding, and settle only the provider-bound canonical order. Provider and account-confirmation regression tests lock the backport.

The SSRF address policy rejects 0.0.0.0/8, unspecified, loopback, link-local, CGNAT, benchmarking, multicast, reserved, IPv4-mapped, and corresponding IPv6 special-purpose ranges. TestSSRFProtectionRejectsLiteralPrivateAndReservedIPs locks each reported bypass family.
